trigger_instruction:
Add unit tests for the file mentioned in the issue.
IF NO TEST FILE EXISTS: - Create a new test file with comprehensive tests for the source file
IF TEST FILE ALREADY EXISTS: - The issue body specifies EXACTLY which lines, functions, and branches are uncovered - Write tests that specifically target those uncovered items (e.g., "Uncovered Lines: 6, 7" means write tests that execute lines 6 and 7) - Use apply_diff_to_file to ADD new test cases - do NOT use replace_remote_file_content - Preserve ALL existing content including headers (eslint-disable, pylint: disable, etc.)
COMMON MISTAKES TO AVOID: - Do NOT assume existing tests are "comprehensive" just because they look thorough - Do NOT replace the entire test file with a "cleaner version" - Do NOT remove or modify existing test file headers - Do NOT just reformat existing tests - you must ADD new ones
